What could be the possible causes of toothache?

Toothache can be caused by several factors, including tooth decay, a dental abscess, gum disease, a cracked tooth, a damaged filling, or even sinusitis. Other causes might include teeth grinding, an impacted wisdom tooth, or sensitivity to hot or cold foods and drinks. It's important to consult a dentist to determine the exact cause and receive appropriate treatment.

Toothache can be caused by various factors, including cavities, gum disease, tooth fracture, a cracked tooth, an exposed tooth root, or a sinus condition.
